description_long: This is a traditional watermill complex featuring a large timber-framed building with a prominent gabled roof and an attached wooden water wheel. The main structure sits on a rectangular base, with a stone or concrete foundation visible at the base and a wooden deck or platform extending from one side. The water wheel is mounted on the side of the building, constructed from wood with visible spokes and paddles, and is positioned over a narrow water channel. Several smaller wooden structures and mechanical components, such as mill machinery and support frames, are arranged adjacent to the main building. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with reddish-brown shingles, while the walls are made of horizontal wooden planks. The overall appearance suggests a historical or rural watermill, likely used for grinding grain or other agricultural processing.
description: A rustic wooden watermill with a large gabled roof, attached water wheel, and several smaller mechanical structures nearby. The building features timber walls, a stone base, and a prominent wooden water wheel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the watermill building, the large water wheel on the left, and several smaller wooden structures aligned in a row.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the gabled entrance of the mill, the water channel, and the arrangement of mechanical components leading up to the building.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the right side of the building, the water wheel now on the right, and the smaller structures in reverse order.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the building with the water wheel centered, showing the water channel and the back of the mill.
attribute_water_wheel: Large overshot wooden water wheel, approximately 2.5 meters in diameter, constructed from timber with 8-10 visible paddles and a spoked hub; features a robust, weathered appearance.
attribute_mill_race: Visible as a narrow open channel leading to the wheel, constructed from wood, approximately 4 meters long and 0.5 meters wide, with a simple trough design.
attribute_building_structure: Single-story rectangular building with a stone or concrete foundation, primary structure made of horizontal timber planks, and a wooden deck/platform; features classic rural mill architecture.
attribute_roof_type: Steeply pitched gabled roof covered with reddish-brown shingles, with extended eaves and no visible chimneys or dormers.
